IRFC shares closed 2.53% lower ahead of the announcement. The stake sale is part of the government’s efforts to raise non-tax revenue and increase public shareholding in public sector companies. In the Union Budget for FY27, the Centre set a target of Rs 80,000 crore through disinvestment and asset monetization. In the starting of this month, the government completed a 5% stake sale in General Insurance Corporation of India (GIC Re), which received strong interest from investors. articles.title: Federal Reserve Board's annual bank stress test confirms that large banks are well positioned to weather a severe recession and able to continue to lend to households and businesses
articles.raw_summary: Federal Reserve Board's annual bank stress test confirms that large banks are well positioned to weather a severe recession and able to continue to lend to households and businesses
articles.extracted_text: An official website of the United States Government Official websites use .gov A .gov website belongs to an official government organization in the United States. Secure .gov websites use HTTPS A lock ( Lock Locked padlock icon ) or https:// means you've safely connected to the .gov website. Share sensitive information only on official, secure websites. The Federal Reserve, the central bank of the United States, provides the nation with a safe, flexible, and stable monetary and financial system. The results of the Federal Reserve Board's annual bank stress test confirmed that large banks are well positioned to weather a severe recession and able to continue to lend to households and businesses. Despite absorbing more than $708 billion in total loan losses under this year's hypothetical scenario, capital declined only 1.6 percentage points in aggregate, staying above minimum capital requirements. "Today's results underscore the strength of the banking system," Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle W. Bowman said. "As we work to increase the transparency and accountability of the stress test, public feedback will help us continue to improve and instill greater confidence in the stress test and its results." As the Board previously announced , today's results will not impact large bank capital requirements, which have been published today. The current capital requirements will stay in place until 2027, when the stress test will be run with loss-estimating models that take public feedback into consideration. All 32 banks tested remained above their minimum common equity tier 1 capital requirements during this year's hypothetical recession scenario, which was similar in severity to the prior test. The hypothetical scenario this year included a severe global recession with a 39 percent decline in commercial real estate prices and a 30 percent decline in house prices. The unemployment rate also increased to a peak of 10 percent, and economic output declined commensurately. Three main factors influenced the results of this year's test, with two leading to a larger decline in the aggregate capital ratio than last year, and one more than offsetting this decline: The total projected losses include roughly $200 billion in credit card losses, $160 billion in losses from commercial and industrial loans, and $75 billion in losses from commercial real estate. For media inquiries, please email [email protected] or call 202-452-2955. 2026 Federal Reserve Stress Test Results (PDF) 2026 Large Bank Capital Requirements (PDF)

articles.title: ECB Consumer Expectations Survey results – May 2026
articles.extracted_text: In May, the median rate of perceived inflation over the previous 12 months remained unchanged at 4.0%. Median expectations for inflation over the next 12 months decreased to 3.5%, from 4.0% in April. Expectations for inflation three years ahead remained unchanged at 2.9% and inflation expectations for five years ahead remained unchanged at 2.4%. Uncertainty about inflation expectations over the next 12 months decreased but remained at a higher level than before the start of the war in the Middle East. Respondents in lower-income quintiles continued to report higher inflation perceptions and expectations, on average, than those in higher-income quintiles. Younger respondents (aged 18-34) continued to report lower inflation perceptions and expectations than older respondents (aged 35-54 and 55-70). Consumers’ nominal income growth expectations over the next 12 months increased to 1.0%, from 0.8% in April. Meanwhile, perceived nominal spending growth over the previous 12 months increased to 5.4%, from 5.3% in April. Expected nominal spending growth over the next 12 months decreased to 3.8%, from 4.3% in April, with respondents in the lowest three income quintiles expecting slightly higher spending growth than those in the highest two quintiles. Economic growth expectations for the next 12 months increased to -1.7%, from -2.2% in April. By contrast, expectations for the unemployment rate 12 months ahead increased to 11.3%, from 11.2% in April. As observed in previous months, lower-income households expected the highest unemployment rate 12 months ahead (13.7%), while higher-income households expected the lowest rate (9.5%). Consumers continued to expect the future unemployment rate to be slightly higher than the perceived current unemployment rate (10.7%), suggesting a broadly stable labour market outlook. In May, consumers expected the price of their home to increase by 3.6% over the next 12 months, compared with 3.7% in April. As in previous months, home price growth expectations in the lowest income quintile (4.1%) remained higher on average than in the highest quintile (3.4%). Expectations for mortgage interest rates over the next 12 months remained unchanged since March at 4.9%. As in previous months, lower-income households expected the highest mortgage interest rates 12 months ahead (5.6%), while higher-income households expected the lowest rates (4.4%). The net percentage of households reporting a tightening (relative to those reporting an easing) of access to credit over the previous 12 months increased further in May, reaching the highest level since February 2024. Meanwhile, the net percentage of households expecting tighter credit conditions (relative to those expecting an easing) over the next 12 months decreased. The release of the CES results for June is scheduled for 24 July 2026. For media queries, please contact: Benoit Deeg , tel.: +49 172 1683704. articles.title: Piero Cipollone: Central bank money for the digital era
ECB to integrate non-financial credit claim portfolios into general collateral framework, phasing out temporary measures
articles.extracted_text: The Governing Council of the European Central Bank (ECB) has decided today on the eligibility criteria and risk control framework to permanently integrate portfolios of non-financial corporate (NFC) credit claims into its general collateral framework. This follows the Governing Council’s initial announcement in November 2024 to phase out temporary collateral measures that allowed banks to post a broader range of NFC credit claims as collateral and marks the final step in phasing out the temporary additional credit claim (ACC) framework. Portfolios of NFC credit claims are composed of the loans that banks grant to non-financial firms in the real economy. Instead of being used individually as collateral, these loans are grouped together by counterparties and presented as a package to obtain liquidity from the Eurosystem. The eligibility criteria, mobilisation and handling practices for the credit claims constituting portfolios of NFC credit claims will be mostly harmonised with the existing criteria for individual credit claims, except for the acceptance of a broader range of credit quality steps. This means that certain loans with a credit quality below the requirement for individual credit claims can be included, as long as they meet diversification criteria within the portfolio, as specified in the applicable risk control framework. The risk control framework complements the eligibility criteria with valuation haircuts and a concentration limit to ensure that the portfolios do not bear a higher risk than assets already eligible under the general collateral framework. Until technical implementation has been completed, existing portfolios of NFC credit claims will continue to be accepted as eligible collateral under the temporary framework. Credit claims benefiting from a COVID-19-related public sector guarantee under the temporary framework will only remain eligible until the end of 2026, unless they meet all requirements of the current general framework. Nonetheless, national central banks may decide to terminate their temporary ACC frameworks earlier. The relevant national central banks will communicate the details to the counterparties affected. Today’s decision marks the return to a single list of eligible collateral applied across the whole euro area, reducing complexity and ensuring a level playing field for all credit institutions. This means the temporary framework can be discontinued, while ensuring that counterparties can still access Eurosystem credit operations using a wide range of collateral under the general framework. Technical implementation is currently planned for November 2027 at the earliest. For media queries, please contact Clara Martín Marqués , tel.: +49 69 1344 17919. articles.title: Isabel Schnabel: Interview with Die Zeit
articles.extracted_text: There is now a ceasefire in the Middle East, and oil prices have fallen. What does this mean for the economy in Europe, and in Germany in particular? This is good news for Europe and the global economy. The war has weighed on growth, and its end could now provide renewed momentum. At the ECB, our primary concern is, of course, the rise in inflation. In recent months, this rise has been driven largely by high energy prices, which have fallen sharply following the preliminary peace agreement. Do you mean, for example, that prices at petrol stations have fallen? Does this mean that the worst of the inflation is behind us? I wouldn’t say that. Energy prices have fallen, but they are still measurably higher than before the war. We are looking in particular at energy prices for deliveries in the coming years, and these remain elevated. Although the short-term situation now looks better than we had expected, the ceasefire is no reason for monetary policymakers to let their guard down. Why are the medium-term energy prices you're talking about not falling more sharply? There are many reasons for this. It is unclear how durable the peace agreement really is. It may also take months before shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and global supply chains return to normal. Transport could permanently remain more expensive, for example due to higher insurance premiums for the Gulf region. So we probably won’t be able to simply go back to the pre-war situation. Energy infrastructure has also been damaged. In addition, the strategic oil reserves that were depleted during the war will need to be replenished. In Europe, gas storage levels are fairly low and need to be refilled before the winter. All of this affects energy prices, and uncertainty remains high. Does the future path of inflation depend solely on energy prices? Not at all. The inflation data for May already show that the energy price shock has broadened. Inflation of non-energy goods and services has already increased, and this is likely at least partly a consequence of the war. The prices of goods that are directly affected by the blockade and by energy prices were the first to react – such as diesel, jet fuel and fertilisers. Surveys show that producers of other goods and services are also planning to pass on part of their sharply higher input costs to consumer prices. This raises the likelihood of second-round effects, in particular stronger wage growth as a result of rising inflation. So far, however, we have seen no evidence that wage growth is accelerating or that people are expecting higher inflation in the long run. But the memory of the high-inflation period is still fresh, so expectations may now respond more strongly to rising inflation. Is it realistic that trade unions will be able to push through excessive wage demands in Germany and other European countries, given the current difficult economic situation? The economy is relatively resilient despite the major energy price shock. According to our projections, growth hasn’t slowed as much as might have been expected. This is also thanks to the AI boom, which has overshadowed the Iran war and is boosting the global economy. At the same time, governments in many countries are spending money to shield their populations from the energy price shock. In Germany in particular, a lot of money is being invested in infrastructure and defence. In addition, the recent fall in oil prices may make it easier for companies to pass on past cost in

articles.extracted_text: China Economic Monitoring and Analysis Center In mid-June 2026, according to the monitoring of market prices of 50 kinds of important means of production under 9 categories in the national circulation field, the prices of 15 products increased, 32 kinds decreased, and 3 kinds remained flat compared with those in early June 2026. Market Prices of Important Means of Production in Circulation Ordinary Hot-Rolled Sheet (4.75-11.5mm, Q235) Pure Benzene (Petroleum Benzene, Industrial Grade) Polyethylene (LLDPE, Melt Index 2 Film Material) Lithium Iron Phosphate (Standard Power Grade) Gasoline (95#, National VI Emission Standard) Diesel Oil (0#, National VI Emission Standard) Shanxi Superior Mixed Coal (5500 kilocalories) Ordinary Portland Cement (P.O 42.5 in Bulk) VII. Market prices of important means of production in circulation refer to the wholesale prices and selling prices of enterprises trading important means of production. Different from producer prices, market prices of means of production include producer prices, as well as circulation expenses, profits, and taxes of the enterprises. Producer prices and market prices influence each other. Due to a time lag, their movements may show divergence in a certain period of time. The monitoring of market prices of important means of production in circulation includes the prices of 50 kinds of products under 9 categories. For details of product categories and specifications, please see the attached table. The monitoring covers nearly 2,000 wholesalers, agents, and dealers of more than 300 trading markets in 31 provinces (autonomous regions and municipalities). Price monitoring methods include information officers gathering prices on-site or requesting prices by telephone, instant messaging tools, e-mail, and so on. The number of products with price increases, decreases, and no change is counted based on the percentage change (%). articles.title: 2.Industrial Production Operation in May 2026
articles.extracted_text: In May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 4.5% year on year in real terms (the growth rates of value added are the real growth rates after deducting price factors), 0.4 percentage points higher than the previous month. On a month-on-month basis, in May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 0.40% over the previous month. From January to May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 5.4% year on year. In terms of three sectors, in May, the value added of the mining industry increased by 2.3% year on year, that of the manufacturing industry increased by 4.4%, and that of the production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water increased by 7.6%. In terms of economic types, in May, the value added of state-holding enterprises increased by 3.7% year on year; that of share-holding enterprises increased by 5.2%; that of enterprises funded by foreign investors and investors from Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan increased by 1.9%; and that of private enterprises increased by 2.7%. In terms of industries, in May, the value added of 28 out of 41 major industries continued to show year-on-year growth. Among them, the value added of the mining and washing of coal went up by 3.5%, that of the extraction of petroleum and natural gas up by 1.5%, that of the processing of food from agricultural and sideline products up by 1.5%, that of the manufacture of liquor, beverages and refined tea down by 2.7%, that of the textile industry up by 2.6%, that of the manufacture of raw chemical materials and chemical products up by 0.3%, that of the manufacture of non-metallic mineral products down by 5.6%, that of the smelting and pressing of ferrous metals up by 1.6%, that of the smelting and pressing of non-ferrous metals down by 4.5%, that of the manufacture of general-purpose machinery up by 6.7%, that of the manufacture of special-purpose machinery up by 9.1%, that of the manufacture of automobiles up by 8.3%, that of the manufacture of railway, ship, aerospace and other transport equipment up by 7.4%, that of the manufacture of electrical machinery and apparatus up by 4.7%, that of the manufacture of computers, communication equipment and other electronic equipment up by 17.0%, and that of the production and supply of electricity and heat power up by 8.7%. In terms of products, in May, the output of 300 out of 626 products produced by industrial enterprises above the designated size increased year on year. Among them, the output of rolled steel was 123.03 million tons, down by 2.8% year on year; that of cement was 149.91 million tons, down by 8.1%; that of ten kinds of non-ferrous metals was 6.98 million tons, up by 2.2%; that of ethylene was 3.38 million tons, up by 2.1%; that of motor vehicles was 2.582 million sets, down by 3.2%, including 1.489 million new energy vehicles, up by 17.8%; the electricity generation was 784.3 billion kWh, up by 4.2%; and the crude oil processing volume was 53.72 million tons, down by 9.1%. In May, the product sales ratio of industrial enterprises above the designated size was 96.0%, a year-on-year decrease of 0.1 percentage points; and the export delivery value realized by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 1,388.4 billion yuan, a nominal increase of 10.1% year on year. articles.title: 3.Investment in Fixed Assets from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: From January to May 2026, the national investment in fixed assets (excluding rural households) was 17,851.2 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 4.1% (calculated on a comparable basis, see Annotation 7 for details). Specifically, the investment in intellectual property products increased by 9.3% year on year. By industry, the investment in the primary industry was 388.7 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 5.9%; the investment in the secondary industry was 6,498.9 billion yuan, up 0.1%; and the investment in the tertiary industry was 10,963.6 billion yuan, down 6.8%. The industrial investment increased by 0.1% year on year. Specifically, the investment in mining increased by 6.2%, the investment in manufacturing decreased by 0.4%, and the investment in production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water decreased by 0.1%. The investment in infrastructure (see Annotation 1 for details of statistical scope) increased by 0.6% year on year. Specifically, the investment in waterway transportation increased by 23.3%, the investment in civil aviation transportation increased by 21.7%, the investment in ecological conservation and environmental governance increased by 3.5%, and the investment in the production and supply of gas increased by 2.8%. By region, the investment in the eastern region decreased by 6.4% year on year, the investment in the central region decreased by 2.9%, the investment in the western region decreased by 6.6%, and the investment in the northeastern region decreased by 17.5%. By statistical category of registration, the investment in fixed assets of domestic-invested enterprises decreased by 3.9% year on year, the investment in fixed assets of enterprises funded by investors from Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan decreased by 8.7%, and the investment in fixed assets of foreign-invested enterprises decreased by 4.3%. From January to May 2026, non-governmental investment in fixed assets decreased by 7.1% year on year. On a month-on-month basis, the investment in fixed assets (excluding rural households) decreased by 1.91% in May. articles.title: 4.Investment in Real Estate Development from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: I. The Completion of Investment in Real Estate Development From January to May, the investment in real estate development was 3,035.6 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 16.2% (calculated on a comparable basis, see Annotation 5 for details), of which the investment in residential buildings was 2,342.6 billion yuan, down 15.6%. From January to May, the floor space of buildings under construction by real estate development enterprises was 5,487.75 million square meters, a year-on-year decrease of 12.3%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings under construction was 3,808.30 million square meters, down 12.6%. The floor space of buildings newly started was 179.29 million square meters, down 22.6%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings newly started was 130.84 million square meters, down 23.4%. The floor space of buildings completed was 140.87 million square meters, down 23.4%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings completed was 99.99 million square meters, down 25.0%. II. Sales of Newly Built Commercial Buildings From January to May, the floor space of newly built commercial buildings sold was 313.20 million square meters, a year-on-year decrease of 10.8%, of which the floor space of residential buildings sold decreased by 12.1%. The sales of newly built commercial buildings were 2,936.6 billion yuan, down 13.5%, of which the sales of residential buildings decreased by 14.1%. At the end of May, the floor space of commercial buildings for sale was 771.82 million square meters, down 0.4% year on year. Specifically, the floor space for sale held for less than three years was 571.52 million square meters, down 2.8%. III. Funds for Investment This Year for Real Estate Development Enterprises From January to May, the funds for investment this year for real estate development enterprises were 3,275.6 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 19.0%. Among them, domestic loans were 487.5 billion yuan, down 28.7%; self-raised funds were 1,198.5 billion yuan, down 13.0%; deposits and advance receipts were 1,001.2 billion yuan, down 16.1%; individual mortgage loans were 406.6 billion yuan, down 28.0%. articles.title: 5.Total Retail Sales of Consumer Goods from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: From January to May, the total retail sales of consumer goods reached 20,603.1 billion yuan, up by 1.4% year on year. Specifically, the retail sales of consumer goods excluding automobiles reached 19,002.2 billion yuan, up by 2.7%. In May, the total retail sales of consumer goods reached 4,109.0 billion yuan, down by 0.6% year on year. Specifically, the retail sales of consumer goods excluding automobiles reached 3,778.1 billion yuan, up by 1.1%. By business area, from January to May, the retail sales of consumer goods in urban areas reached 17,866.2 billion yuan, up by 1.2% year on year; those in rural areas were 2,736.9 billion yuan, up by 2.6%. In May, the retail sales of consumer goods in urban areas reached 3,574.1 billion yuan, down by 0.9% year on year; those in rural areas were 534.9 billion yuan, up by 1.5%. By consumption pattern, from January to May, the retail sales of goods were 18,254.3 billion yuan, up by 1.2% year on year; the income of the catering industry was 2,348.8 billion yuan, up by 3.1%. In May, the retail sales of goods were 3,648.5 billion yuan, down by 0.7% year on year; the income of the catering industry was 460.5 billion yuan, up by 0.6%. By retail format, from January to May, the retail sales of convenience stores and supermarkets among retail enterprises (units) above the designated size increased by 6.8% and 3.6% year on year, respectively; those of specialty stores, department stores and brand-exclusive stores decreased by 1.2%, 1.8% and 7.6%, respectively. From January to May, Chinaâs online retail sales of goods and services reached 8,317.7 billion yuan, up by 5.9% year on year. Specifically, the online retail sales of goods were 5,271.8 billion yuan, up by 5.0%; among the online retail sales of goods, those of food, clothing, and daily necessities increased by 15.5%, 7.2%, and 1.6%, respectively. The online retail sales of services were 3,045.9 billion yuan, up by 7.6%. articles.title: 6.Energy Production in May 2026
articles.extracted_text: In May 2026, the raw coal production by industrial enterprises above the designated size remained at a relatively high level, the growth of crude oil production was steady, the growth of natural gas production fell slightly, and the growth of electricity generation accelerated. I. Raw Coal, Crude Oil and Natural Gas Production and Related Information Raw coal production remained at a relatively high level. In May, the raw coal production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 400 million tons, down by 1.7% year on year; the average daily output was 12.81 million tons. From January to May, the raw coal production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 1.98 billion tons, a year-on-year decrease of 0.3%. The growth of crude oil production was steady. In May, the crude oil production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 18.57 million tons, a year-on-year increase of 0.5%, 0.7 percentage points lower than that in April; the average daily output was 599 thousand tons. From January to May, the crude oil production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 91.31 million tons, a year-on-year increase of 1.1%. The decline in the processing volume of crude oil expanded. In May, the processing volume of crude oil by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 53.72 million tons, a year-on-year decrease of 9.1%, with the decline expanded by 3.3 percentage points from April; the average daily processing volume was 1.733 million tons. From January to May, the processing volume of crude oil by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 292.80 million tons, a year-on-year decrease of 2.2%. Natural gas production fell slightly. In May, the production of natural gas by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 21.7 billion cubic meters, a year-on-year decrease of 2.2%, compared with a 1.9% increase in April; the average daily output was 700 million cubic meters. From January to May, the production of natural gas by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 111.7 billion cubic meters, a year-on-year increase of 1.7%. Electricity generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size grew faster. In May, electricity generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 784.3 billion kWh, a year-on-year increase of 4.2%, with the growth rate 1.6 percentage points higher than that in April; the average daily electricity generation was 25.30 billion kWh. From January to May, electricity generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 3,912.9 billion kWh, a year-on-year increase of 3.6%. By category, in May, the growth of thermal power generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size slowed down, while that of hydropower and solar power generation accelerated; the growth of nuclear power and wind power generation turned from decline to growth. Among them, thermal power generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 2.1% year on year, with the growth rate 1.0 percentage point lower than that in April; hydropower generation increased by 13.0%, with the growth rate 0.8 percentage points higher than that in April; nuclear power generation increased by 5.0%, compared with an 8.7% decline in April; wind power generation increased by 0.5%, compared with a 5.0% decline in April; solar power generation increased by 12.1%, with the growth rate 5.0 percentage points higher than that i

articles.title: 7.National Economy Showed Steady Momentum with Innovation-led and High-quality Developmen...
articles.extracted_text: In May, under the strong leadership of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China (CPC) with Comrade Xi Jinping at its core, all regions and departments fully implemented the decisions and arrangements made by the CPC Central Committee and the State Council, adhered to the general principle of pursuing progress while ensuring stability, fully and faithfully applied the new development philosophy on all fronts, moved faster to foster a new pattern of development, strictly implemented more proactive and effective macro policies, and effectively addressed external shocks and challenges. As a result, production and supply registered steady progress, employment and prices were generally stable, foreign trade demonstrated strong resilience, and new growth drivers witnessed robust development. The national economy maintained steady momentum with innovation-led and high-quality development. 1. Industrial Production Witnessed Accelerated Growth and Equipment Manufacturing and High-Tech Manufacturing Grew Fast. In May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size grew by 4.5 percent year on year, 0.4 percentage points faster than that of the previous month; the month-on-month growth was 0.40 percent. In terms of sectors, the value added of mining increased by 2.3 percent year on year, that of manufacturing grew by 4.4 percent, and that of production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water went up by 7.6 percent. The value added of equipment manufacturing increased by 9.5 percent year on year and that of high-tech manufacturing increased by 15.1 percent, 1.2 percentage points and 2.3 percentage points faster than that of the previous month respectively. An analysis by types of ownership showed that the value added of state holding enterprises was up by 3.7 percent year on year; that of share-holding enterprises was up by 5.2 percent; that of enterprises funded by foreign investors or investors from Hong Kong, Macao and Taiwan was up by 1.9 percent; and that of private enterprises was up by 2.7 percent. In terms of products, the production of 3D printing devices, lithium-ion batteries and industrial robots grew by 54.4 percent, 40.0 percent and 27.9 percent year on year respectively. In the first five months, the value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size went up by 5.4 percent year on year. In May, the Manufacturing Purchasing Managersâ Index stood at 50.0 percent. The Production and Operation Expectation Index was 53.9 percent. In the first four months, the total profits made by industrial enterprises above the designated size were 2,435.8 billion yuan, up by 18.2 percent year on year. 2. Service Sector Grew Steadily and Modern Services Showed Good Momentum of Development. In May, the Index of Services Production grew by 4.4 percent year on year, 0.1 percentage points faster than that of the previous month. Specifically, that of information transmission, software and information technology services, leasing and business services, finance, and transport, storage and postal services grew by 11.3 percent, 10.9 percent, 7.0 percent and 4.8 percent year on year respectively. In the first five months, the Index of Services Production increased by 4.8 percent year on year. In the first four months, the business revenue of service enterprises above the designated size grew by 6.4 percent year on year. In May, the Business Activity Index for Services stood at 50.3 percent and the

articles.title: 8.Sales Prices of Commercial Residential Buildings in 70 Medium and Large-sized Cities in...
articles.extracted_text: Sales Price Indices of Newly Constructed Commercial Residential Buildings in 70 Large and Medium-Sized Cities Sales Price Indices of Second-Hand Residential Buildings in 70 Large and Medium-Sized Cities Price Indices of Newly Constructed Commercial Residential Buildings by Floor Space in 70 Large and Medium-Sized Cities Price Indices of Second-Hand Residential Buildings by Floor Space in 70 Large and Medium-Sized Cities 1. Survey Coverage: The survey was conducted in the municipal districts of 70 medium and large-sized cities, excluding the counties. 2. Survey Methods: The survey of newly constructed commercial residential buildingsâ sales prices is an overall survey, with basic data derived from online contract-signing data provided by local real estate management departments. The survey of second-hand residential buildingsâ sales prices combines the survey of main units with typical survey, with basic data sourced from reports submitted by real estate agencies or housing service platforms, as well as on-site price collection by surveyors. 3. The calculation of the price indices can be seen in the Statistical Reporting System on Real Estate Prices. 4. If there is no deal record this month, the overall price level is regarded as unchanged. 5. According to the requirements of the statistical system, starting from January 2026, the statistical survey of real estate prices in 70 medium and large-sized cities takes 2025 as the base period for a new round of comparison. The weights of basic categories for each city have been adjusted to better reflect changes in the residential building sales structure. According to the calculation, the impact of the base period rotation on the monthly year-on-year sales price indices of commercial residential buildings in 70 medium and large-sized cities is within 0.03 percentage points on average.

articles.title: 10.Consumer Price Index in May 2026
articles.extracted_text: In May 2026, Chinaâs Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by 1.2% year on year. Specifically, the price index in urban areas increased by 1.3% while that in rural areas increased by 1.1%; the price index for food decreased by 1.7% while that for non-food increased by 1.9%; the price index for consumer goods increased by 1.6% while that for services increased by 0.8%. From January to May, on average, Chinaâs CPI increased by 1.0% year on year. In May, Chinaâs CPI decreased by 0.1% month on month. Specifically, the price index in urban areas decreased by 0.1% while that in rural areas decreased by 0.1%; the price index for food decreased by 0.4% while that for non-food decreased by 0.1%; the price index for consumer goods decreased by 0.2% while that for services decreased by 0.1%. I. Year-on-Year Changes in Price Indexes for Various Goods and Services In May, the price index for food, tobacco, liquor and dining out decreased by 0.9% year on year, bringing the CPI down by about 0.26 percentage points. Among food, the price index for meat of livestock decreased by 7.4%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.31 percentage points, of which the price index for pork decreased by 16.1%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.31 percentage points; that for fresh fruits decreased by 2.2%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.04 percentage points; that for eggs increased by 6.6%, pushing the CPI up by about 0.03 percentage points; that for fresh vegetables increased by 1.6%, pushing the CPI up by about 0.02 percentage points; that for aquatic products increased by 0.6%, pushing the CPI up by about 0.01 percentage points. Among the price indexes for the other seven major categories, six increased, and one decreased year on year. Specifically, the price indexes for miscellaneous goods and services, transportation and telecommunication, and health care and medical services increased by 9.9%, 5.4% and 2.1%, respectively; the price indexes for household facilities, articles and services, clothing and education, culture and recreation increased by 1.8%, 1.4% and 1.3%, respectively; the price index for residence decreased by 0.2%. II. Month-on-Month Changes in Price Indexes for Various Goods and Services In May, the price index for food, tobacco, liquor and dining out decreased by 0.2% month on month, bringing the CPI down by about 0.06 percentage points. Among food, the price index for fresh vegetables decreased by 3.6%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.06 percentage points; that for meat of livestock decreased by 0.8%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.03 percentage points, of which the price index for pork decreased by 1.6%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.03 percentage points; that for eggs increased by 5.0%, pushing the CPI up by about 0.03 percentage points; and that for fresh fruits increased by 0.6%, pushing the CPI up by about 0.01 percentage points. Among the price indexes for the other seven major categories, one increased, four decreased and two remained flat month on month. Specifically, the price index for clothing increased by 0.6%; the price indexes for education, culture and recreation, and health care and medical services remained flat; the price indexes for household facilities, articles and services and miscellaneous goods and services both decreased by 0.4%; the price indexes for transportation and telecommunication and residence decreased by 0.3% and 0.1%, respectively. articles.title: 11.Industrial Producer Price Indexes in May 2026
articles.extracted_text: In May 2026, Chinaâs producer price index for industrial products (PPI) increased by 3.9% year on year and 0.5% month on month. The purchasing price index for industrial producers increased by 5.8% year on year and 1.3% month on month. From January to May, on average, the producer price index for industrial products increased by 1.0% year on year, and the purchasing price index for industrial producers increased by 1.6%. I. Year-on-Year Changes in Industrial Producer Price Indexes In May, among producer price indexes for industrial products, the price index for means of production increased by 5.2% year on year, pushing the overall producer price index for industrial products up by about 4.08 percentage points. Specifically, the price index for mining and quarrying increased by 15.8%, that for raw materials increased by 9.2%, and that for the processing industry increased by 2.3%. The price index for consumer goods decreased by 0.8%, bringing the overall producer price index for industrial products down by about 0.18 percentage points. Specifically, the price index for food decreased by 1.8%, that for clothing and articles for daily use both decreased by 1.0%, and that for durable consumer goods remained flat. Among purchasing price indexes for industrial producers, the price index for nonferrous metals and cables increased by 22.0%, that for raw chemical materials increased by 11.8%, that for fuel and power increased by 10.0%, that for textile materials increased by 2.5%, that for ferrous metals increased by 0.3%, that for building materials and non-metals decreased by 5.5%, and that for agricultural and sideline products decreased by 1.6%. II. Month-on-Month Changes in Industrial Producer Price Indexes In May, among producer price indexes for industrial products, the price index for means of production increased by 0.7% month on month, pushing the overall producer price index for industrial products up by about 0.53 percentage points. Specifically, the price index for mining and quarrying increased by 1.5%, that for raw materials increased by 0.9%, and that for the processing industry increased by 0.5%. The price index for consumer goods remained flat. Specifically, the price index for food decreased by 0.1%, and that for clothing, articles for daily use and durable consumer goods all increased by 0.1%. Among purchasing price indexes for industrial producers, the price index for raw chemical materials increased by 4.2%, that for fuel and power increased by 2.7%, that for textile materials increased by 1.0%, that for ferrous metals increased by 0.6%, that for nonferrous metals and cables and for agricultural and sideline products both increased by 0.3%, and that for building materials and non-metals both decreased by 0.5%. articles.title: Address to school graduates
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International President of Russia Vladimir Putin: Dear graduates, friends, I sincerely congratulate you, your families, and your teachers on this wonderful day of your graduation. School graduation is always filled with a unique and exciting atmosphere, charged with the energy of youth, hope, and dreams. The bigger part of your life is connected with your school: important events, emotions, and experiences. For many of you, it has truly become a second home. Bidding farewell to school is a special and deeply moving moment, the memories of which you will cherish for years to come. I join you in expressing heartfelt gratitude to your teachers. Throughout your years at school, they opened up the boundless world of knowledge to you, supported you during difficult moments, and helped you discover and develop your talents and abilities. They did everything they could to ensure you grew up to be good, decent people. This is an extraordinarily demanding and profoundly important mission. Very soon, it is you, the young people of today, who will assume responsibility for our country, our beautiful and beloved Russia. It will be your mission to continue the history of its achievements, to create, to build, and to leave a legacy that you, in turn, will pass on to future generations. Today, you are looking forward to a future that is still unknown, trying to imagine who you will become in the years ahead and what achievements you will have. I believe you will be able to fulfil your plans and accomplish your goals. You possess the gene of winners. Within your hearts and souls live the strength and unity of our people, along with the moral fortitude of your ancestors, those who liberated the world from Nazism, harnessed the power of the atom, and opened the way to outer space for all humanity. You will also face difficult and inspiring challenges in the spheres of technology, science, public administration, social development, culture, and the arts. The most important thing is to pursue your goals with determination and perseverance. And, of course, always remember the most important lessons you learned at school: the lessons of friendship, kindness, justice, courage, and mutual support. Cherish your love for your homeland, look to its heroes for inspiration, and uphold the timeless values that have always been the foundation of our nation. A world of opportunity now lies before you, and how you make use of it depends, above all, on you. I wish each of you success in gaining admission to the educational institution of your choice. Or perhaps you have decided to master a profession through vocational training and begin your career right away. Whatever path you choose, the most important thing is to find your true calling, never stop learning, continue to grow, and always strive to move forward. In today’s rapidly changing world, this is more important than ever. Dream and dare! I wish you happiness, fulfilment, and every success. Published in sections: News , Transcripts

articles.title: Address to participants in the 13th Forum of Russian and Belarusian Regions
articles.raw_summary: Vladimir Putin delivered a video address to the participants in the 13 th Forum of Regions of Russia and Belarus.
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Vladimir Putin delivered a video address to the participants in the 13 th Forum of Regions of Russia and Belarus. President of Russia Vladimir Putin : Ms Kochanova, Ms Matviyenko, friends, I am delighted to send my greetings to the participants and organisers of the 13 th Forum of Regions of Russia and Belarus which takes place, as usual, ahead of the Independence Day of the Republic of Belarus. Celebrated on July 3, it marks the anniversary of Minsk’s liberation from Nazi invaders. Let me convey my heartfelt congratulations to all our friends in Belarus on this national holiday. Russia will always remember that our two nations stood shoulder to shoulder during the Great Patriotic War and endured immense sacrifices to defend their freedom and independence, as well as to bring peace to Europe and the entire humankind. Importantly, Russia and Belarus have been cherishing and expanding their traditions of brotherly friendship and mutual support while reinforcing their historical, cultural and spiritual bonds. Russia and Belarus have forged a genuinely strategic relationship, and the Union State we form serves as a model of mutually beneficial and equal integration. It makes a tangible contribution to deepening cooperation within associations such as the Eurasian Economic Union, the Commonwealth of Independent States, and the Collective Security Treaty Organisation. Region-to-region ties serve as one of the main drivers of Russia’s partnership with Belarus and efforts to develop the Union State. They offer a solid foundation for steadily advancing cooperation between our two countries across various tracks, including in politics and security, social and humanitarian affairs, and in the economy, of course. Held regularly, the forums of regions bring together representatives of federal and local governments, legislators and business leaders, as well as experts and civil society activists – all those who are committed to establishing and developing direct contacts between Russian and Belarusian regions. This year, over 600 delegates from 73 regions of the Russian Federation arrived in the capital of Belarus, including governors, heads of regional legislative assemblies, members of parliament, ministers, and corporate executives. Belarus has an equally prominent delegation at the forum. A solid package of agreements and business contracts signed during the forum demonstrates our shared commitment to achieving tangible results. The topic you are discussing – Economic Cooperation of Regions – Foundation for Sustainable Development of Russia and Belarus – is extremely relevant in today’s world. Let me note that it is largely thanks to close region-to-region economic ties that we were able to set a new record in our bilateral trade last year. According to our statistics, it was equal to US$52 billion. This year, this growth pattern is set to continue with a 7 percent increase in the first quarter. Our countries have been carrying out all their transactions in national currencies. There has been a proactive effort to harmonise laws governing trade, investment and finance. We are unifying our approaches to customs and tax administration and are introducing a unified quality control framework. The Union State Committee for Standardisation and Quality held its first meeting during this forum. Russia is second to none in terms of accumulated investment in the Belar

articles.title: Greetings on the opening of the professional skills competition for disabled veterans of the special military operation
articles.raw_summary: Vladimir Putin sent a message of greetings to the participants and guests of the professional skills competition for disabled veterans of the special military operation.
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Vladimir Putin sent a message of greetings to the participants and guests of the professional skills competition for disabled veterans of the special military operation. “I extend my greetings on the occasion of the opening in Kazan of the professional skills competition for participants of the special military operation who, having sustained severe injuries, nevertheless have refused to yield and continue to faithfully work in service to the Motherland. Your competition occupies a truly distinguished place among the significant undertakings dedicated to supporting participants and veterans of combat operations. It serves as a vital platform, enabling our heroes to fully showcase their talents and capabilities, to expand their professional horizons, and to acquire new competencies. I am confident that the extensive business, cultural, sporting, and exhibition programme, coupled with the atmosphere of fair competition and the opportunity to interact with accomplished experts and like-minded individuals, will provide you with every opportunity to excel, gain invaluable experience, forge ahead with assurance, and realise the objectives you have set for yourselves. Furthermore, you will undoubtedly be able to secure reliable partners for your future projects and initiatives. Friends, we take immense pride in your courage, valour, and fortitude, and in your unwavering commitment to selflessly serving the Fatherland. I wish you every success in your endeavours and undertakings, and in the fulfilment of all your plans.”

articles.title: Greetings to participants, guests and organisers of the Philosophy of the Future: Concepts and Meanings international research-to-practice conference
articles.raw_summary: Vladimir Putin sent greetings to the participants, guests and organisers of the Philosophy of the Future: Concepts and Meanings international research-to-practice conference.
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Vladimir Putin sent greetings to the participants, guests and organisers of the Philosophy of the Future: Concepts and Meanings international research-to-practice conference. “The conference, aimed at facilitating a serious and substantial discussion of humanitarian and civilisational issues of critical importance for the future of Russia and humanity at large, brought together leading scientists, experts, and representatives of think tanks and higher education institutions from Russia and abroad at the Vorobyovy Gory Innovation Science and Technology Centre of Lomonosov Moscow State University. At a time of rapid technological, social, and cultural transformation, the significance of the humanities continues to grow. There is an increasing need for thoughtful and responsible philosophical reflection on contemporary developments and challenges. As we consider the future, we seek to understand how people will experience life in a digitally driven world, how our perception of life will evolve, and the principles that will shape relations among individuals and cultures. In this context, I would like to emphasise that your meeting offers participants an excellent opportunity to engage in meaningful academic dialogue and to exchange views in an open and respectful manner.”

articles.title: Greetings to Merited Master of Sports in Figure Skating and Merited Coach of the USSR Tamara Moskvina
articles.raw_summary: Vladimir Putin sent his greetings to Merited Master of Sports in Figure Skating and Merited Coach of the USSR Tamara Moskvina on her birthday.
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Vladimir Putin sent his greetings to Merited Master of Sports in Figure Skating and Merited Coach of the USSR Tamara Moskvina on her birthday. “You rightfully belong to the remarkable galaxy of great athletes, coaches, and innovators whose achievements have enhanced the prestige of the national school of figure skating. Your brilliant and distinctive talent, coupled with your mastery and dedication, enabled you to distinguish yourself at national championships and Spartakiads, as well as on the international stage. Furthermore, your many years of devoted coaching and your unwavering commitment to nurturing successive generations of athletes – many of whom have gone on to attain true global acclaim – command the utmost respect. It is truly gratifying that, throughout the decades, you have retained your youthful spirit, inspiration, and generosity in sharing your talent, maintained an active and creative outlook, and remain highly sought after to this day, enjoying well-earned and considerable authority.”

articles.title: Greetings to the participants, organisers and guests of the 15th International Ballet Competition
articles.raw_summary: Vladimir Putin sent greetings to the participants, organisers and guests of the 15 th International Ballet Competition, held in memory of Yury Grigorovich, an outstanding choreographer.
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Vladimir Putin sent greetings to the participants, organisers and guests of the 15 th International Ballet Competition, held in memory of Yury Grigorovich, an outstanding choreographer. “Launched back in 1969, this competition has acquired a truly legendary status as one of the oldest, most important and spectacular highlights on the international cultural scene. Our country has always been famous for its rich choreographic traditions and has every right to be proud of them today. This is why talented dancers from many countries nurture ambitious and daring dreams of connecting with the great history of Russian ballet by performing at the Bolshoi Theatre. Of course, taking part in this prestigious competition is a great opportunity for young dancers to get into the spotlight and receive support from experienced teachers and mentors, while also discovering our capital and its landmarks. I am convinced that this anniversary competition will take place in an atmosphere of creativity and mutual understanding and will help us discover new talent as a celebration of beauty for all those who appreciate it.”

articles.title: Meeting with permanent members of the Security Council
articles.raw_summary: Vladimir Putin held a briefing session, via videoconference, with permanent members of the Security Council.
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Vladimir Putin held a briefing session, via videoconference, with permanent members of the Security Council. Taking part in the meeting were Prime Minister Mikhail Mishustin Mishustin Mikhail Prime Minister of the Russian Federation , State Duma Speaker Vyacheslav Volodin Volodin Vyacheslav Chairman of the State Duma , Deputy Chairman of the Security Council Dmitry Medvedev Medvedev Dmitry Deputy Chairman of the Security Council of the Russian Federation , Chief of Staff of the Presidential Executive Office Anton Vaino Vaino Anton Chief of Staff of the Presidential Executive Office , Presidential Aide Nikolai Patrushev Patrushev Nikolai Aide to the President , Defence Minister Andrei Belousov Belousov Andrei Defence Minister of the Russian Federation , Interior Minister Vladimir Kolokoltsev Kolokoltsev Vladimir Interior Minister of the Russian Federation , Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov Lavrov Sergei Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Russian Federation , Director of the Federal Security Service Alexander Bortnikov Bortnikov Alexander Director of the Federal Security Service , and Director of the Foreign Intelligence Service Sergei Naryshkin Naryshkin Sergei Director of the Foreign Intelligence Service . President of Russia Vladimir Putin: Colleagues, good afternoon. There are two items on the agenda today. The first pertains to the safeguarding of internal security and stability. The second concerns our relations – the development of our relations with our immediate neighbours. I propose that we begin with the domestic agenda. We have two speakers: Director of the Federal Security Service Alexander Bortnikov and Interior Minister Vladimir Kolokoltsev. Published in sections: News , Transcripts , Security Council
